subject: Geography
title: Straight Lines in the Middle East
description: A quick win: understand Straight Lines in the Middle East.

hook.text: Jordan's eastern border is a straight line drawn by Winston Churchill after a long lunch.

buildup.text: Churchill later joked he created Jordan with 'one stroke of a pen on a Sunday afternoon.'

discovery.text: The Sykes-Picot line sliced through communities that had traded together for centuries.

twist.text: Bedouin tribes suddenly found their grazing routes crossing international borders.

climax.text: Lines drawn for imperial convenience became fault lines for a century of conflict.

punchline.text: Empires love straight lines. People living on them do not.

quiz.question: Who famously joked about creating Jordan 'on a Sunday afternoon'?
